title: CLAUDE.md PubId section says base64 but code uses hex(SHA-256)
description: Trigger: A contributor reads CLAUDE.md Section 6 ('PubId comparison and base64 encoding'). It states: 'The string is always standard base64 (base64::engine::general_purpose::STANDARD, alphabet A-Za-z0-9+/, padded). Do not use URL_SAFE or URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.' Bad behavior: The actual implementation uses hex(SHA-256(verifying_key_bytes)) -- 64 lowercase hex characters. identity.rs:hash_key() uses format!("{b:02x}"). Test pub_id_is_hash_not_key() asserts pub_id.0.len()==64 and chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()). Any external client or contributor following CLAUDE.md will encode PubIds as 44-char base64 strings that will never match the relay's 64-char hex PubIds, producing auth failures with no helpful diagnostic. The CLAUDE.md was not updated when the PubId scheme changed from raw-key-base64 to hash-of-key-hex.
